notes.text: see context obj type capture logic. at present this is incapable of distinguishing "chinPhrase" (actual chinese phrases of more than one word) and "chinWord" (a single word unit in chinese). to address this, we want to develop additional functionality for the codex worker: 1) we will ensure that existing funcctionality remains intact, callable and properly wired; 2) we will set up additional functionality by which when the user sends an ambiguous chinPhrase type context (which may be chinWord) it is properly distinuished and passed back, being updated on saved data in the background (with UI reading the update as it is saved so it can make use of the disambiguated context type); 3) toggling on/off of this ffunctionality ("chin disambiguation) will be controlled by a new option on setttings list. checking the option's checkbox will turn it on.

we are to edit data captured with 
"context" property value as follows: it will be an obj with props including current value string as "text". a second property "type" will be determined for context - either chinPhrase, chinFuzzWord, engPhrase or engWord. defintion guidelines are as follows: 1) chinPhrase will consist of chinese mandarin characters, punctuation and other symbols, and english characters (but only insofar as they correspond to possible chinese yinyin (e.g. hello !== pinyin but "haluo" does)). if entire context is english characters but corresponds to possible pinyin, then type is "chinFuzzWord". note that pinyin can optionally include digits 1-5 after a pinyin syllable (denoting tone mark); 2) engPhrase will consist of primarily english characters as well as optional punctuation and other symbols, and must contain one or more space characters. if no spaces but comprised of english characters (optional symbols and punctuation included) and does not consist only of subStrings that correspond to pinyin, then it is of type "engWord";

rename the subSeg bubble on subSeg input to the langUnit bubble

let the ethereal seed item display without border, min height max-content/auto and aligned to right.
let the user cycle selection of list item by ctrl+arrowup/down keypress;

cycle selection targeting on list is displaying target indiccation on wrong itme index

replacedBy: index of items in list should be based on actual display order

notes.text: cycle tareting needs to be able to target all indexes. deafults to target index -1 on page load
index of items in list  should be based on actual display order
cycle targeting should cycle from top to bottom of list (visual order of appearance top to bottom) - at perssent it is working in reverse

replacedBy: ctrl+arrowup/down selection uses visual top-to-bottom cycling now

notes.text: space keypress on cycle target udpates time element as if audio is playing but im not hearing any audio

replacedBy: current audio playback handling with real audio elements and playback labels

notes.text: the page cannot handle two concurrent audio streams. update to ensure that they hand off properly

ctrl+backspace on targeted audEp triggers item confirm delete dialogue. while confrm dialogue open, targeting style changes color to red. let dialogue be contained as a second state on item with "cancel" and "confirm" labelled buttons corresponding to actions. auto-focus cancel option. tab press or ctrl arrow right/left cycles between options
non-augmented (no ctrl hold, shift hold, etc) arrow left/right keypress while targeting audEp seeks audio playback
seek is +-5sec depending on direction
seek +5 sec on arrow right, -5 sec on arrow left
ctrl+backspace from entered state collapses the entered state container and unlocks it
initialize a data collection for audSegs in C:\retry\src\backend\data
while in entered state, ctrl+arrow left/right is used to cycle select targeting on audSeg items

while an audEp is in entered state, ctrl+arrowup/down will not cycle audEp targeting, but instead jump audSeg cycle targeting across rows, e.g. audSeg cycle targeting index 0 ctrl+down keypress will jump to target audSeg index 3, etc
ctrl+backspace no longer triggers delete dialogue but instead sets audEp cycle target to index -1 ; let delete keypress on cycle target tigger delete dialogue
audSeg list not centering in dev properly. fix this. it should have equal padding either side

notes.text:
ctrl+arrow left/right while focused to input will cycle targeting of bubbles on subSeg input ccontent. cycle target style is blue border (same color as other cycle selects); 
ctrl+shift+left/right arrow and left right arrow keypresses are not to be impacted by this new keypress behavior
while subSeg bubble currently being cycle targeted, ctrl+backspace will first set cycling to -1. only if no cycle target active will exit audSeg trigger
while subSeg cycle target active, input loses cursor. when cycle target is -1, it is restored to the end of the input

while subSeg cycle target active, normal text input is disabled on the input- note that other keypresses used to control bubble cycle and selection are unaffected. once cycle target is -1, normal input works again
debug: save is not persisting new lines on input rich text through reloads
when all content deleted off of subSegInput, subsequent inputted input is treated as being within the subSeg bubble span. fix this so that after delete, it behaves as default
let input content wrap once it reaches row width

notes.text:
enter keypress while a substring is highlighted on input content text will trigger "capture subSeg" action - for the time being this action will only place the substring within a "bubble" stye span that wraps the subString with a pseudo  container span;
this will not block or otherwise interfere with plain lone enter keypress behavior on input;

rapid space keypress *2 on either extreme index of the subSeg bubble span will break the cursor free of the bubble and replace the resulting double space characters with a single space character that is not within the span bounds;

enter press while targeting audSeg  will lock to that target and clamp audio playback to its start and end times (playback wraps), also seeking to its start time

audSeg items will reorganize as follows when an enter press locks an audSeg to entered state: 
1) IF the entered audSeg is not the first audSeg, it will be moved to occupy its own row alone. its width will become the entire row. audSegs after it will be moved to continue as normal on following row(s). If it is the first audSeg, it will fill the current row and others will be moved down. If it is the last audSeg, it will be moved down to its own row and will occupy the whole row. when entered state exited, the default 3-cards per row will be restored.
